questions.question: What is the diameter of a powerlifting bar according to IPF specifications?
questions.explanation: The diameter of the bar must not exceed 29mm or be less than 28mm.
questions.ruleQuote: "Diameter of the bar is not to exceed 29 mm or be less than 28 mm."

questions.question: What is the total weight of the bar and collars in IPF competitions?
questions.explanation: The weight of the bar and collars are to be 25kg.
questions.ruleQuote: "Weight of the bar and collars are to be 25kg."

questions.question: What is the distance between the 81cm marks on the bar used for bench press grip measurement?
questions.explanation: There shall be a diameter machined marking to measure 81cm between markings.

questions.question: What is the maximum weight of competition discs in IPF competitions?
questions.explanation: Discs must be within the following range: 1.25kg, 2.5kg, 5kg, 10kg, 15kg, 20kg, and 25kg.
questions.ruleQuote:
"Discs must be within the following range: 1.25 kg, 2.5 kg, 5 kg, 10 kg, 15 kg, 20 kg, and 25kg.

For record purposes, lighter discs may be used to achieve a weight of at least 0.25 kg 0.5 kg 1.0 kg, 1.5 kg or 2 kg more than the existing record."

questions.question: What color must 25kg discs be according to IPF rules?
questions.explanation: Discs must conform to the following color code: 25kg - red, 20kg - blue, 15kg - yellow, 10kg and under - any color.
questions.ruleQuote:
Discs must conform to the following color code: 10kg and under - any color, 15kg - yellow, 20kg - blue,
25kg - red.

questions.question: What color must 20kg discs be according to IPF rules?
questions.explanation: Discs must conform to the following color code: 20kg - blue.
questions.ruleQuote:
Discs must conform to the following color code: 10kg and under - any color, 15kg - yellow, 20kg - blue,
25kg - red.

questions.question: What color must 15kg discs be according to IPF rules?
questions.explanation: Discs must conform to the following color code: 15kg - yellow.
questions.ruleQuote:
Discs must conform to the following color code: 10kg and under - any color, 15kg - yellow, 20kg - blue,
25kg - red.

questions.question: How much must each collar weigh?
questions.explanation: Collars must weigh 2.5kg each.
questions.ruleQuote:
Collars
(a) Shall always be used.
(b) Must weigh 2.5 kg each.

questions.question: What is the minimum height range for squat rack adjustment?
questions.explanation: The squat racks shall be designed to adjust from a minimum height of 1.00m in the lowest position to extend to a height of at least 1.70m in 2.5cm increments.

questions.question: What is the minimum length of the competition bench?
questions.explanation: The bench length shall not be less than 1.22m and shall be flat and level.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench

1. Length - not less than 1.22 m and shall be flat and level.

questions.question: What is the width range of the bench pad?
questions.explanation: The bench width shall not be less than 29cm and not exceeding 32cm.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench

2. Width - not less than 29 cm and not exceeding 32cm.

questions.question: What is the height range of the bench from the floor?
questions.explanation: The bench height shall not be less than 42cm and not exceeding 45cm measured from the floor to the top of the padded surface.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench

3. Height - not less than 42 cm and not exceeding 45 cm measured from the floor to the top of the padded surface of the bench without it being depressed or compacted. The height of the uprights, which must be adjustable, shall be a minimum of 75 cm to a maximum of 110 cm measured from the floor to the bar rest position.

questions.question: What color light indicates a successful lift in IPF competition?
questions.explanation: White light represents a 'good lift' and red light represents 'no lift'.
questions.ruleQuote:
Lights

A system of lights shall be provided whereby the referees make known their decisions. Each referee will control a white and a red light. These two colors represent a “good lift” and “no lift” respectively.

questions.question: What is the minimum squat depth required for a good lift?
options: Top of legs at hip below top of knees
questions.explanation: The lifter must bend the knees and lower the body until the top surface of the legs at the hip joint is lower than the top of the knees.

questions.question: What is the Chief Referee's command to start the squat?
questions.explanation: The signal shall consist of a downward movement of the arm and the audible command 'Squat'.
questions.ruleQuote:
Squat

2. After removing the bar from the racks, (the lifter may be aided in removal of the bar from the racks by the spotter / loaders) the lifter must move backwards to establish the starting position. When the lifter ismotionless, erect (slight deviation is allowable) with kneeslocked the Chief Referee will give the signal to begin the lift. The signal shall consist of a downward movement of the arm and the audible command “Squat”. Before receiving the signal to “squat” the lifter may make any position adjustments within the rules, without penalty. For reasons of safety the lifter will be requested to “Replace” the bar, together with a backward movement of the arm, if after a period of five seconds he is not in the correct position to begin the lift. The Chief Referee will then conveythe reason why the signal was not given.

questions.question: What is the Chief Referee's command to end the squat?
questions.explanation: The signal to rack the bar will consist of a backward motion of the arm and the audible command 'Rack'.
questions.ruleQuote:
Squat

5. The signal to rack the bar will consist of a backward motion of the arm and the audible command “Rack”. The lifter must then return the bar to the racks. Foot movement after the rack signal will not be cause for failure. For reasons of safety the lifter may request the aid of the spotter/loaders in returning the bar to, and replacing it in the racks. The lifter must stay with the bar during this process. The lifter is not allowed to walk out through the front of the rack after completion of the lift.

questions.question: How many spotter/loaders can be on the platform at any time?
questions.explanation: Not more than five and not less than two spotter/loaders shall be on the platform at any time.
questions.ruleQuote:
Squat

6. Not more than five and not less than two spotter/loaders shall be on the platform at any time. The Referees may decide to the number of spotter/loadersrequired on the platform at any time 2, 3, 4, or 5.

questions.question: Is double bouncing at the bottom of the squat allowed?

ruleReference.subsection: Causes for Disqualification of a Squat

questions.explanation: Double bouncing at the bottom of the lift, or any downward movement during the ascent causes disqualification.
questions.ruleQuote:
Causes for Disqualification of a Squat

2. Double bouncing at the bottom of the lift, or any downward movement during the ascent.

questions.question: What must be in contact with the bench during the bench press?
questions.explanation: The lifter must lie on his back with head, shoulders, and buttocks in contact with the bench surface.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench Press

2. The lifter must lie on his back with head, shoulders, and buttocks(the picture PROPER STARTING POSITION& SETUP show the minimum acceptable) in contact with the bench surface. The feet must be flat on the floor (as flat as the shape of the shoe will allow). His hands and fingers must grip the bar positioned in the rack stands with a thumb around grip. This position shall be maintained throughout the lift. Foot movement is permissible but must remain flat on the platform. During the set-up on the bench, the athlete is not allowed to place his/her feet on the bench. The hair must not hide the back of the head when lying down on the bench. The Jury or Referees may require the lifter to affix his/her hear accordingly.

questions.question: What is the maximum grip width allowed on bench press?
questions.explanation: The spacing of the hands shall not exceed 81cm measured between the forefingers.

questions.question: Is the reverse grip allowed in bench press?
questions.explanation: The use of the reverse grip is forbidden.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench Press

5. The spacing of the hands shall not exceed 81 cm measured between the forefingers (both forefingers must be within the 81 cm marks and the whole of the forefingers must be in contact with the 81 cm marks if maximum grip is used). The use of the reverse grip is forbidden.

questions.question: What is the Chief Referee's command to start the bench press?
questions.explanation: The signal to begin the attempt shall consist of a downward movement of the arm together with the audible command 'Start'.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench Press

7. The signal to begin the attempt shall consist of a downward movement of the arm together with the audible command “Start”.

questions.question: What command is given after the bar is motionless on the chest in bench press?
questions.explanation: After the bar is held motionless on the chest, the Chief referee will signal the audible command 'Press'.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench Press

8. After receiving the signal, the lifter must lower the bar to the chest or abdominal area whereby the underside of both elbow joints is lowered level with or below the top surface of each respective shoulder joint (the bar shall not touch the belt), hold it motionless, after which the Chief referee will signal the audible command “Press “The lifter must then return the bar to straight arms’ length elbows locked. When held motionless in this position the audible command “Rack” shall be given together with a backward motion of the arm. If the baris lowered to the belt or does not touch the chest or abdominal area, the Chief Referee´s command is “Rack”.

questions.question: What is required for the bar position on the chest during bench press?
options: Bar must be held motionless with visible pause
questions.explanation: The lifter must lower the bar to the chest or abdominal area and hold it motionless with a definite and visible pause.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench Press

8. After receiving the signal, the lifter must lower the bar to the chest or abdominal area whereby the underside of both elbow joints is lowered level with or below the top surface of each respective shoulder joint (the bar shall not touch the belt), hold it motionless, after which the Chief referee will signal the audible command “Press“. The lifter must then return the bar to straight arms’ length elbows locked. When held motionless in this position the audible command “Rack” shall be given together with a backward motion of the arm. If the bar is lowered to the belt or does not touch the chest or abdominal area, the Chief Referee's command is “Rack”.

questions.question: Can the bar touch the belt during bench press?
questions.explanation: The bar shall not touch the belt. If the bar is lowered to the belt, the Chief Referee's command is 'Rack'.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench Press

8. After receiving the signal, the lifter must lower the bar to the chest or abdominal area whereby the underside of both elbow joints is lowered level with or below the top surface of each respective shoulder joint (the bar shall not touch the belt), hold it motionless, after which the Chief referee will signal the audible command “Press“. The lifter must then return the bar to straight arms’ length elbows locked. When held motionless in this position the audible command “Rack” shall be given together with a backward motion of the arm. If the bar is lowered to the belt or does not touch the chest or abdominal area, the Chief Referee's command is “Rack”.

questions.question: What maximum height are foot blocks allowed for bench press?
questions.explanation: The lifter may use flat surfaced plates, or blocks not exceeding 30cm in total height to build up the surface of the platform.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench Press

3. To achieve firm footing the lifter may use flat surfaced plates, or blocks not exceeding 30 cm in total height and a minimum dimension of 60 cm x 40 cm, to build up the surface of the platform. Blocks in the range of 5 cm, 10 cm, 20 cm, and 30 cm, should be made available for foot placement at all international competitions.

questions.question: What is the minimum foot block dimension required at international competitions?
questions.explanation: Foot blocks should have a minimum dimension of 60cm x 40cm.
questions.ruleQuote:
Bench Press

3. To achieve firm footing the lifter may use flat surfaced plates, or blocks not exceeding 30 cm in total height and a minimum dimension of 60 cm x 40 cm, to build up the surface of the platform. Blocks in the range of 5 cm, 10 cm, 20 cm, and 30 cm, should be made available for foot placement at all international competitions.

questions.question: What is the command to end the deadlift?
questions.explanation: The Chief Referee's signal shall consist of a downward movement of the arm and the audible command 'Down'.
questions.ruleQuote:
Deadlift

3. The Chief Referee’s signal shall consist of a downward movement of the arm and the audible command “Down”. The signal will not be given until the bar is held motionless and the lifter is in the apparent finished position.

questions.question: Is there a start command for the deadlift?
questions.explanation: For deadlift commencement, no signal is required.
questions.ruleQuote:
Deadlift - Commencement

No signal required

questions.question: What position must the lifter be in at the completion of the deadlift?
options: Standing erect with knees locked and shoulders back
questions.explanation: On completion of the lift the knees shall be locked in a straight position and the shoulders back.
questions.ruleQuote:
Deadlift

2. On completion of the lift the knees shall be locked in a straight position and the shoulders back.

questions.question: Is supporting the bar on the thighs allowed during deadlift?

ruleReference.subsection: Causes for Disqualification of a Deadlift

questions.explanation: Supporting the bar on the thighs during the performance of the lift causes disqualification. If the bar edges up the thigh but is not supported, this is not reason for disqualification.
questions.ruleQuote:
Causes for Disqualification of a Deadlift

4. Supporting the bar on the thighs during the performance of the lift. If the bar edges up the thigh but is not supported this is not reason for disqualification. The lifter should benefit in all decisions of doubt made by the referee.

questions.question: Is any downward movement of the bar allowed during deadlift?
options: No, until final position is reached
questions.explanation: Once the attempt has begun no downward movement is allowed until the lifter reaches the erect position with the knees locked. If the bar settles as the shoulders come back, this should not be reason to disqualify the lift.

questions.question: What is the maximum belt width allowed?
questions.explanation: Width of belt maximum 10cm.
questions.ruleQuote:
Belt - Dimensions

1. Width of belt maximum 10 cm.

questions.question: What is the maximum belt thickness?
questions.explanation: Thickness of belt maximum 13mm along the main length.
questions.ruleQuote:
Belt - Dimensions

2. Thickness of belt maximum 13 mm along the main length.

questions.question: Is wearing a belt mandatory in IPF competition?
questions.explanation: Competitors may wear a belt. If worn, it shall be on the outside of the lifting suit.
questions.ruleQuote:
Belt

Competitors may wear a belt. If worn, it shall be on the outside of the lifting suit. Only belts from manufacturers accepted onto the “Approved List of Apparel and Equipment for Use at IPF Competitions” shall be permitted for use in competitions.

questions.question: What is the maximum wrist wrap length?
questions.explanation: Wrist wraps shall not exceed 1m in length and 8cm in width.

questions.question: What is the maximum wrist wrap width?
questions.explanation: Wrist wraps shall not exceed 1m in length and 8cm in width.
questions.ruleQuote:
Wraps - Wrists

1. Wrists wraps shall not exceed 1 m in length and 8 cm in width. Any sleeves and Velcro patches/tabs for securing must be incorporated within the one-meter length. A loop may be attached as an aid to securing. The loop shall not be over the thumb or fingers during the actual lift. in width. A combination of wrist wraps and sweat bands is not allowed

questions.question: What is the maximum total wrist covering width?
questions.explanation: A wrist covering shall not extend beyond 10cm above and 2cm below the center of the wrist joint and shall not exceed a covering width of 12cm.
questions.ruleQuote:
Wraps - Wrists

2. A wrist covering shall not extend beyond 10 cm above and 2 cm below the center of the wrist joint and shall not exceed a covering width of 12 cm.

questions.question: What is the maximum knee wrap length in equipped competitions?
questions.explanation: Wraps not exceeding 2m in length and 8cm in width may be worn only in competitions which are designated as Equipped.
questions.ruleQuote:
Wraps - Knees

1. Wraps not exceeding 2 m in length and 8 cm in width may be worn only in competitions which are designated as Equipped. A knee wrap shall not extend beyond 15 cm above and 15 cm below the center of the knee joint and shall not exceed a total covering width of 30 cm. IPF approved knee sleeves are allowed. A combination of the two is strictly forbidden. Neoprene may be “synthetic rubber, but is only acceptable in the knee sleeve.

questions.question: What is the maximum total knee wrap coverage width?
questions.explanation: A knee wrap shall not extend beyond 15cm above and 15cm below the center of the knee joint and shall not exceed a total covering width of 30cm.
questions.ruleQuote:
Wraps - Knees

1. Wraps not exceeding 2 m in length and 8 cm in width may be worn only in competitions which are designated as Equipped. A knee wrap shall not extend beyond 15 cm above and 15 cm below the center of the knee joint and shall not exceed a total covering width of 30 cm. IPF approved knee sleeves are allowed. A combination of the two is strictly forbidden. Neoprene may be “synthetic rubber, but is only acceptable in the knee sleeve.

questions.question: What is the maximum knee sleeve thickness?
questions.explanation: Knee sleeves shall be of a maximum thickness of 7mm and a maximum length of 30cm.
questions.ruleQuote:
Wraps - Knees

(b) Knee sleeves shall be of a maximum thickness of 7 mm and a maximum length of 30 cm.

questions.question: What is the maximum knee sleeve length?
questions.explanation: Knee sleeves shall be of a maximum thickness of 7mm and a maximum length of 30cm.
questions.ruleQuote:
Wraps - Knees

(b) Knee sleeves shall be of a maximum thickness of 7 mm and a maximum length of 30 cm.

questions.question: Can knee wraps and knee sleeves be worn together?
questions.explanation: A combination of the two is strictly forbidden.
questions.ruleQuote:
Wraps - Knees

1. Wraps not exceeding 2 m in length and 8 cm in width may be worn only in competitions which are designated as Equipped. A knee wrap shall not extend beyond 15 cm above and 15 cm below the center of the knee joint and shall not exceed a total covering width of 30 cm. IPF approved knee sleeves are allowed. A combination of the two is strictly forbidden. Neoprene may be “synthetic rubber, but is only acceptable in the knee sleeve.

questions.question: What must be worn to protect shins during deadlift?
questions.explanation: Shin length socks must be worn to cover and protect the shins while performing the deadlift.
questions.ruleQuote:
Socks

(c) Full length leg stockings, tights or hose are strictly forbidden.
Shin length socks must be worn to cover and protect the shins while performing the deadlift.

questions.question: Are full length leg stockings allowed?
questions.explanation: Full length leg stockings, tights or hose are strictly forbidden.
questions.ruleQuote:
Socks

(c) Full length leg stockings, tights or hose are strictly forbidden.
Shin length socks must be worn to cover and protect the shins while performing the deadlift.

questions.question: What is the maximum shoe sole height?
questions.explanation: No part of the underside shall be higher than 5cm.
questions.ruleQuote:
Shoes or Boots

(b) No part of the underside shall be higher than 5 cm.

questions.question: How many attempts is each lifter allowed per lift?
questions.explanation: Each competitor is allowed three attempts on each lift.
questions.ruleQuote:
GENERAL RULES OF POWERLIFTING

(e) Each competitor is allowed three attempts on each lift. The lifter’s best valid attempt on each lift counts toward his competition total. If two or more lifters achieve the same total, the lighter lifter ranks above the heavier lifter.

questions.question: In what order must the lifts be performed in competition?
questions.explanation: The IPF recognizes the following lifts which must be taken in the same sequence: Squat, Bench Press, Deadlift.
questions.ruleQuote:
GENERAL RULES OF POWERLIFTING

(a) The International Powerlifting Federation recognizes the following lifts which must be taken in the same sequence in all competitions conducted under IPF rules: A. Squat B. Bench Press C. Deadlift D. Total

questions.question: How is ranking determined when two lifters achieve the same total?
questions.explanation: If two or more lifters achieve the same total, the lighter lifter ranks above the heavier lifter.
questions.ruleQuote:
GENERAL RULES OF POWERLIFTING

(e) Each competitor is allowed three attempts on each lift. The lifter’s best valid attempt on each lift counts toward his competition total. If two or more lifters achieve the same total, the lighter lifter ranks above the heavier lifter.

questions.question: If two lifters weigh the same and achieve the same total, who wins?
options: The one who achieved total first
questions.explanation: If two lifters register the same bodyweight and achieve the same total, the lifter making the total first will take precedence.
questions.ruleQuote:
GENERAL RULES OF POWERLIFTING

(f) If two lifters register the same bodyweight at the weigh in and eventually achieve the same total at the end of the competition, the lifter making the total first will take precedence over the other lifter. Where awards are presented for best squat, bench press and deadlift or if a world record is broken, the same procedure will apply.

questions.question: How many referees officiate each lift?
questions.explanation: The referees shall be three in number, the Chief Referee and two side referees.

questions.question: What is required for a lift to be considered successful?
questions.explanation: A majority decision (at least two white lights) is required for a successful lift.

questions.question: How long is the weigh-in period?
questions.explanation: The weigh in period will last one and a half hours.

questions.question: How early before competition does weighing-in take place?
questions.explanation: Weighing in must take place no earlier than two hours before the start of the competition.

questions.question: How many times can a lifter be weighed officially?
questions.explanation: Each lifter may only be weighed once. Only those whose bodyweight is heavier or lighter than the category limits are allowed to return to the scales.

questions.question: How much time does a lifter have to start their attempt after being called?
questions.explanation: The lifter is allowed one minute in which to start his attempt after the Speaker calls the lifter to the bar.

questions.question: How much time does a lifter have to submit their next attempt after completing a lift?
questions.explanation: A lifter must submit his second and third attempts within one minute of completing his preceding attempt.

questions.question: What is the minimum weight increase between attempts?
questions.explanation: The progression must be at least 2.5kg between all attempts, unless attempts are made on records.

questions.question: What is the minimum increase required to set a new record?
questions.explanation: In a record attempt the weight of the barbell must be at least 0.5kg in excess of the current record.

questions.question: How many changes are permitted on the third attempt deadlift?
questions.explanation: In the third round of the deadlift, two changes are permitted.

questions.question: Can second and third attempt weights be changed after submission?
questions.explanation: Weights submitted for second round attempts cannot be changed. Similarly, third round attempts on the squat and bench press cannot be changed.

questions.question: What happens if a lifter fails three attempts on any lift?
questions.explanation: Three unsuccessful attempts in any lift will automatically eliminate the lifter from the overall competition. He may still compete for awards on individual lifts.

questions.question: What is the age range for Sub-Junior category?
questions.explanation: Sub-Junior: from the day he reaches 14 years and throughout the full calendar year in which he reaches 18 years.

questions.question: What is the age range for Junior category?
questions.explanation: Junior: from 1 January in the calendar year he reaches 19 years and throughout the full calendar year in which he reaches 23 years.

questions.question: At what age does the Open category begin?
questions.explanation: Open: from 1 January in the calendar year he reaches 19 years and upwards.

questions.question: What is the age range for Master I category?
questions.explanation: Master I: from 1 January in the calendar year he reaches 40 years and throughout the full calendar year in which he reaches 49 years.

questions.question: At what age does Master IV category begin?
questions.explanation: Master IV: from 1 January in the calendar year he reaches 70 years and upwards.

questions.question: What is the minimum platform size according to IPF rules?
questions.explanation: All lifts shall be carried out on a platform measuring between 2.5m x 2.5m minimum and 4.0m x 4.0m maximum.

questions.question: What is the maximum platform height from the floor?
questions.explanation: It must not exceed 10cm in height from the surrounding stage or floor.

questions.question: Is rubber matting allowed on the platform surface?
questions.explanation: The surface of the platform must be covered with a material of non-slip smooth carpet. Rubber matting or similar sheeting materials are not permitted.

questions.question: What is the total overall length limit for a powerlifting bar?
questions.explanation: Total overall length not to exceed 2.2m.

questions.question: What is the distance between collar faces on the bar?
questions.explanation: Distance between the collar faces is not to exceed 1.32m or be less than 1.31m.

questions.question: What is the sleeve diameter range?
questions.explanation: Diameter of the sleeve not to exceed 52mm or be less than 50mm.

questions.question: What is the maximum diameter of the largest disc?
questions.explanation: The diameter of the largest discs shall not be more than 45cm.

questions.question: How must the heaviest discs be loaded on the bar?
questions.explanation: The first and heaviest discs loaded on the bar must be loaded face in; with the rest of the discs loaded face out.

questions.question: How many Jury members are there at World Championships?
questions.explanation: The Jury shall consist of three or five Category 1 referees.

questions.question: Can the Jury reverse a referee's decision?
options: Only by unanimous vote for 3 member jury
questions.explanation: Only in extreme circumstances when there has been an obvious or blatant mistake in the refereeing will the Jury by unanimous jury vote with 3 Member Jury may reverse the decision.

questions.question: What is the passing score for Category 2 referee written examination?
questions.explanation: Must achieve a passing score of 85 percent or more on written examination and 85 percent or more on practical examination.

questions.question: What is the passing score for Category 1 referee examination?
questions.explanation: The candidate must score at least 90 percent on his total examination.

questions.question: How long must someone be a Category 2 referee before upgrading to Category 1?
questions.explanation: Must be a Category 2 referee in good standing for a period of at least 4 years.

questions.question: Is the use of oil or grease on the body allowed?
questions.explanation: The use of oil, grease or other lubricants on the body, or personal equipment is strictly forbidden.

questions.question: Which substances can be applied to the body?
options: Baby powder, resin, talc, magnesium carbonate
questions.explanation: Baby powder, resin, talc or magnesium carbonates are the only substances that may be added to the body and attire, but not to the wraps.

questions.question: Is adhesive allowed on the underside of footwear?
questions.explanation: The use of any form of adhesive on the underside of footwear is strictly forbidden.

questions.question: Must suit straps be worn over the shoulders?
options: Yes, at all times in all lifts
questions.explanation: The suit-straps must be worn over the lifter's shoulders at all times in all lifts in all competitions.

questions.question: What is the minimum leg length of a lifting suit?
questions.explanation: There must be legs to the suit, extending a minimum of 3cm from the top of the crotch down the inside of the leg.

questions.question: What is the maximum leg length of a supportive lifting suit?
questions.explanation: There must be legs to the supportive suit, extending a minimum of 3cm and a maximum of 15cm.

questions.question: What is the maximum leg length of a non-supportive lifting suit?
questions.explanation: There must be legs to the non-supportive suit, extending a minimum of 3cm and a maximum of 25cm.

questions.question: Where must the t-shirt sleeves terminate?
options: Below the deltoid but not at/below elbow
questions.explanation: Those sleeves must terminate below the lifter's deltoid and must not extend onto or below the lifter's elbow.

questions.question: Can knee sleeves touch the socks?
options: No, must not be in contact
questions.explanation: When worn by the lifter in competition, knee sleeves must not be in contact with the lifter's suit (other than long legged) or socks.

questions.question: How many layers of medical tape may be worn around the thumbs?
questions.explanation: Two layers of medical tape may be worn around the thumbs.

questions.question: Are hats allowed on the platform during lifting?
questions.explanation: Hats are strictly forbidden to be worn on the platform during lifting.

questions.question: Is wearing a Hijab allowed during competition?
questions.explanation: Lifters may wear Hijab (head scarf) while lifting.

questions.question: What red card (failure card #1) indicates for squat?
questions.explanation: Red card (failure 1) for squat: Failure to bend the knees and lower the body until the top surface of the legs at the hip joint is lower than the top of the knees.

questions.question: What blue card (failure card #2) indicates for squat?
options: Double bouncing or downward movement during ascent
questions.explanation: Blue card (failure 2) for squat: Failure to assume an upright position with knees locked, or double bouncing/any downward movement during the ascent.

questions.question: What yellow card (failure card #3) indicates for squat?
questions.explanation: Yellow card (failure 3) for squat: Stepping backward or forward or moving feet laterally, or failure to observe Chief Referee's signals.

questions.question: What is the minimum uprights height range for bench press?
questions.explanation: The height of the uprights shall be a minimum of 75cm to a maximum of 110cm measured from the floor to the bar rest position.

questions.question: What is the minimum width between bar rests on the bench?
questions.explanation: Minimum width between insides of bar rests shall be 1.10m.

questions.question: How far must the head of the bench extend beyond the center of uprights?
questions.explanation: The head of the bench shall extend 22cm beyond the center of the uprights with a tolerance of 5cm either way.

questions.question: Is rocking feet between ball and heel permitted in squat?

ruleReference.subsection: Causes for Disqualification of a Squat

questions.explanation: Stepping backward or forward or moving the feet laterally causes disqualification. Rocking the feet between the ball and heel is permitted.

questions.question: Can foot movement occur after the 'Down' command in deadlift?

ruleReference.subsection: Causes for Disqualification of a Deadlift

questions.explanation: Foot movement after the command 'Down' will not be cause for failure.
